title: World History: Key Events
description: Explore pivotal moments in world history, from ancient civilizations to the 20th century.

quiz.prompt: In which year did World War II end?
quiz.explanation: World War II ended in 1945: Germany surrendered on May 8 (V-E Day) and Japan surrendered on September 2 (V-J Day).

quiz.prompt: Which of the following were causes of World War I?

options.text: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand

quiz.explanation: WWI was caused by nationalism, imperial rivalry, the assassination of Franz Ferdinand, and complex alliance systems. The Great Depression came later and contributed to WWII.

quiz.front: What was the French Revolution (1789) and why is it significant?
quiz.back: The French Revolution was a period of radical political and social transformation in France. It overthrew the monarchy, established a republic, and promoted ideas of liberty, equality, and fraternity. It influenced democratic movements worldwide and marked the decline of feudal privilege.

quiz.prompt: Which empire built the Colosseum in Rome?
quiz.explanation: The Roman Colosseum (Flavian Amphitheatre) was built under the Roman Empire, completed in 80 AD under Emperor Titus.

quiz.back: The Cold War (1947-1991) was a period of geopolitical tension between the United States and Soviet Union. It featured an arms race, space race, proxy wars, and ideological conflict between capitalism and communism, but never resulted in direct military confrontation between the superpowers.

steps.title: Week 1: Ancient Civilizations
steps.description: Explore Greece, Rome, and early empires.

steps.title: Week 2: Modern Revolutions
steps.description: Study the French Revolution and its global impact.

steps.title: Week 3: 20th Century Conflicts
steps.description: Understand WWII and the Cold War.

tasks.title: Essay: Compare WWI and WWII causes
